concept.title: Using a WireGuard client behind NAT and firewalls

conbody.p: WireGuard uses the UDP protocol and transmits data only when a peer sends packets. Stateful firewalls and network address translation (NAT) on routers track connections to enable a peer behind NAT or a firewall to receive packets.

To keep the connection active, WireGuard supports persistent keepalives. This means you can set an interval at which WireGuard sends keepalive packets. By default, the persistent keep-alive feature is disabled to reduce network traffic. Enable this feature on the client if you use the client in a network with NAT or if a firewall closes the connection after some time of inactivity.

note.p: Note that you cannot configure keepalive packets in WireGuard connections by using the RHEL web console. To configure this feature, edit the connection profile by using the nmcli utility.
